sections.paragraph: to bow oneself down, to prostrate oneself, to lay oneself down. 2Ki 4:34, 35 2Ki 4:35, used of Elisha in the raising of the dead child, עָלָיו יִּגְהַר “and he bowed himself upon him.” 1Ki 18:42, וַיִּגְהַר אַרְצָה “and he cast himself down on the ground.” This signification, which the context almost demands, is expressed by all the ancient interpreters (except the Ch. and Arabic, 2 Ki.). The Syriac has the same word under the letters ܓܗܢܶ Ethpeal, to which answers the Ch. גְּחַן; see examples of the interchange of the letters ר and ן under the letter Nun.
